summary: Remove DropIndexesResult
description:
In SWIFT-702 we decided to add a {{DropIndexesResult}} type.

However, for {{MongoDatabase.drop}} and {{MongoCollection.drop}}, we went with the "returning void" approach. 

We should remove the index result type for consistency with the other {{drop}} methods.

summary: Introduce protocol(s) for SDAM monitoring events
description: Since we're introducing the {{MongoCommandEvent}} protocol that the command monitoring events conform to, it may make sense to introduce similar ones for SDAM monitoring to allow access to shared fields across events without casting to concrete types. This could also allow for easier filtering by users and in testing.

summary: Gracefully handle errors parsing isMaster responses for SDAM monitoring
description:
Currently, our code for constructing a {{ServerDescription}} for publishing in an SDAM monitoring event assumes the description is well-formed - for ex. that the host-port String is formatted as host:port. 

We do some force unwrapping, and a lot of casting/field lookups that may silently fail. 

We might consider refactoring to better handle errors, and decide how we should handle publishing SDAM events where we run into such errors: do we skip them? do we publish them with a missing field? etc. 

I think the server description parsing could probably also be rewritten to better take advantage of BSONDecoder.

summary: Consider introducing a syncClose method on async client
Users should provide a callback rather than a NotificationCenter for APM
description:
Right now we force users to consume APM events from the default NotificationCenter for their app.

We should instead have users provide a custom callback we will execute when an event happens

summary: Add options assertions in initial DNS seedlist discovery spec tests
description: If/when CDRIVER-3328 is done, we can implement the options assertions in the initial DNS seedlist discovery spec tests.

summary: listIndexNames should extract names from Documents rather than decoding entire IndexModels
description:
Currently, {{listIndexNames}} is implemented by mapping over the models returned from {{listIndexes}} and extracting the name fields.
This requires us to pay the overhead for each name of decoding the entire {{IndexModel}}. 
Instead we should take an approach similar to that in {{listCollectionNames}} where we create a cursor over {{Document}} s and just extract the name fields.

summary: Avoid roundtripping values through Swift when copying values from one document to another
Fix potential for deadlock when all connections are in use
description:
A deadlock scenario can occur in the driver if all of the connections are currently checked out and held by types that own their own connections (e.g. cursors) and all the threads in the thread pool become blocked on operations waiting for connections to become available.

The cursors cannot make progress iterating because they need free threads, so they never finish and release their connections, blocking the threads in the threadpool indefinitely.

summary: Condense TLSOptions into ClientOptions struct, and make property names consistent with URI options spec
description: To mirror the flat structure of connection strings we should merge everything into the ClientOptions struct and make the names match those from the URI options spec.

summary: Use Self rather than type(of: self)
description: As of Swift 5.1 these are equivalent and the first is a bit more readable.

summary: Provide default values for enum associated values where appropriate
description:
Swift 5.1 adds support for default values for enum associated values.
We should do this where it is appropriate in our API, e.g. for the options provided to various bulk write cases.

summary: Test that readPreferenceTags are always interpreted as an array
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    We currently lack coverage for behavior when users provide only a single {{readPreferenceTags}} option in the connection string.
    See DRIVERS-989 for updated details.

summary: Add RetryableWriteError error labels to retryable transaction tests
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    Mongos currently doesn't support filling in errorLabels on a failCommand fail point, this adds them explicitly to the retryable transaction tests.
    See DRIVERS-826 for updated details.

summary: Add 'allowDiskUse' option to find command
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    Update find() API in drivers to add allowDiskUse support.
    See DRIVERS-694 for updated details.

summary: Standardize on how we handle properties that we want to nil out in deinit methods
description:
When a type has a property whose value is set to something definitively non-nil in the init method, and is then nil-ed out in the deinit, and is otherwise untouched, how should we represent it?

This typically arises in cases where we want to destroy references to pointers/types wrapping pointers. 

I think we have two options basically:

1) Give the property an optional type. When we use it, we then have to unwrap it first if it is being passed into a Swift method. (If passed into a libmongoc method, libmongoc will force-unwrap it.) This is the approach we currently use for the properties in MongoClient, MongoDatabase, MongoCollection.

2) Give the property a forced type. This basically means that even though a default value isn't provided, we promise the compiler that the value will always be set before we try to use it. This is the approach we currently use for the bson_t in DocumentStorage.

summary: Update BSONValue to more safely be encoded to Documents
description:
As [~kaitlin.mahar] points out in [this thread|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/pull/264#discussion_r285219884], the {{encode(to:forKey)}} method on {{BSONValue}} circumvents the newly introduced memory safe internal API. We should avoid this.

One suggestion is to modify {{BSONValue}} to no longer mutate the document at all, but to instead have an accessor that would provide the data to be encoded to the document. See the above linked thread for some discussion on that subject.

summary: Support for 'authorizedDatabases' option
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    {panel:title=Useful Info|borderStyle=dashed|borderColor=#ccc|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2} 
h4.Summary
Add support for a new parameter `authorizedDatabases` to database enumeration spec.

*Lead*: Jeff
*Author*: Robert
*POCs*:

*Relevant Spec Changes*: 

{panel}
    See DRIVERS-685 for updated details.

summary: Consolidate BSON pointer access
description:
Right now we provide two ways of accessing a pointer to a {{Document}}'s underlying {{bson_t}}:
 * the free floating {{withMutableBSONPointer(to:\)}} function for mutable access
 * the {{_bson}} property on {{Document}} for immutable access

For consistency's sake, we should probably move these to be methods on {{Document}} itself, and both have {{withXPointer}} names. See {{[ContiguousArray|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/contiguousarray]}} for an example of something doing this in Foundation.

One unfortunate thing is that these two methods, because they operate on {{OpaquePointer}} s post Swift 5.0, don't actually enforce the mutability restrictions they claim. They do signal intent though, which is a step in the right direction.

summary: Use Int in public API as much as possible
description:
Even in cases where negative values don't really make sense, the Swift community seems to have converged on using {{Int}} as much as possible.

For example, {{NIOThreadPool.init(numberOfThreads:\)}} takes in an {{Int}}, as does {{MultiThreadedEventLoopGroup.init(numberOfThreads:\)}}. 

Also, from the [Swift language guide|https://docs.swift.org/swift-book/LanguageGuide/TheBasics.html] section on {{UInt}}:
bq. Use {{UInt}} only when you specifically need an unsigned integer type with the same size as the platform’s native word size. If this isn’t the case, {{Int}} is preferred, even when the values to be stored are known to be nonnegative. A consistent use of {{Int}} for integer values aids code interoperability, avoids the need to convert between different number types, and matches integer type inference, as described in Type Safety and Type Inference.

We've followed this guidance for more recently introduced parameters e.g. {{maxPoolSize}} and {{threadPoolSize}} but we likely have not followed it everywhere in the API. We should perform an audit and correct any places that we could be using {{Int}} but are not.

summary: Suppress "ns not found" errors in MongoCollection.drop()
description:
See [mongodb/mongo-swift-driver#97|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/pull/97#discussion_r215791133] and https://mongodbcr.appspot.com/245090001/ for context.

It looks like error code 26 was introduced in MongoDB 3.2, so supporting earlier versions of MongoDB will requiring inspecting the error message for "ns not found". That said, suppressing these errors for MongoDB 3.0 may not be worth the effort.

summary: localizedDescription of errors should contain error "message"
description: The localizedDescription field of our various error types should contain the {{message}} field of an error, if there is one. Not all of our errors satisfy this though, which leads to us having to extract the messages manually in the transactions tests. We should update any errors that do contain a message to relay it through the localizedDescription. For errors with multiple messages, we should consider concatenating them in some way.

summary: More convenient API for ReadConcern/WriteConcern creation
description:
We updated our {{ReadPreference}} API in SWIFT-738 to have static properties and methods for more convenient creation. We should do similarly for {{ReadConcern}} and maybe also {{WriteConcern}}.

{{ReadConcern}} should now have:
 * public static properties named {{local}}, {{available}}, {{linearizable}}, {{majority}}, {{snapshot}} 
 * a static method {{other}} accepting a string, that can be used for forward compatibility if the server adds a new level 
* no public initializer accepting a level
* possibly, no public initializer at all, and a {{default}} static property that corresponds to the empty {{ReadConcern}} 

{{WriteConcern}} will need some more investigation as it has various options, but at the least we could add static properties for common cases like {{majority}}.

summary: Investigate issue with 5.2 release mode C interop
Provide Transactions example for Docs
description: See DRIVERS-488 for details.

summary: Standardize on conversion method names
description:
In the new BSON library, we're introducing a {{toData}} method rather than a {{.data}} computed property to convey that a copy is being made. We should standardize on this approach both to signal intent and to aid in discoverability of conversion mechanisms.

This mostly applies to converting the various {{asX}} methods on {{BSON}} to {{toX}}.

summary: Fix thread sanitizer warnings
description:
Running our tests with the thread sanitizer via {{swift test --sanitize thread}} we get several warnings, most of which seem to stem from our passing a {{MongoClient}} as the context for mongoc APM callbacks. We should investigate and fix these prior to 1.0. 

We should also try running the sanitizer with the simple benchmark app since that will cover more multithreaded scenarios than our tests do.

summary: Rename "tag" W case to "custom"
description:
The official docs have settled on the term "custom write concern name" for the non-majority string value for \{{w}} in a WriteConcern. We should update our field name to reflect that, as "write tag" isn't a commonly understood term.

see: [https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/write-concern/#writeconcern.%3Ccustom-write-concern-name%3E]

summary: Add prefixes to public type names where needed
Rewrite enums with ".other" case as structs
description:
Given that there is no syntactical difference between using static properties vs enums outside of switching, our current non-exhaustive enums (those that include an .other case for forward compatibility) should probably be rewritten as structs. The enum with {{.other}} case strategy does provide forward compatibility, but users will be relegated to using {{.other("some value")}} until the next _major_ version bump, whereas with static properties, new values can be added in _minor_ versions.

A survey of the driver for enums that have {{.other}} case yields:
-CollectionType: will be handled in a separate ticket

 * FullDocument: The spec specifically requires that users be able to specify unknown values
 ** Proposal: convert to struct with a static updateLookup, and allow FullDocument to be initializable from String (and string literal)

-ReadConcern.Level: was handled via SWIFT-815

 [relevant discussion|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/pull/459#discussion_r416031904]

summary: Rewrite extJSON properties as functions
description: The computed properties {{canonicalExtJSON}} and {{extJSON}} on {{BSONDocument}} can be quite expensive. We should rewrite these as functions to convey that.

summary: allowDiskUse option for find should be documented as only being supported in 4.4+
description: See DRIVERS-1193 for updated details.

summary: We should be able to exhaustively switch on BSONValue types
description:
We have no way to exhaustively enumerate all the possible BSONValue types.

This has caused bugs in the past and causes bugs like SWIFT-508.

We should think about a way to fix this.

One option is making {{BSONValue}} an enum rather than a protocol, with a case for each BSON type and an associated value for each case. However this may be problematic for things like integers where multiple Swift types map to single BSON types.

We might be able to just come up with some internal way to list all the types and ensure we keep that type updated. We already have such a list in the tests {{AllBSONTypes}} but have not yet come up with a way to ensure it includes instances of all possible {{BSONValue}} types.

summary: Add validation to BSON construction
description: Add validation logic to BSON Document constructors to ensure the incoming BSON is correct. A non-exhaustive list of properties to ensure would be: size, no duplicate keys, ending null byte on keys and document, and type byte indicators are valid.

summary: Make BSON Documents Mutable
description: Allow users to change BSON documents, implement the setters via key. This includes the dynamic member lookup and subscript with default functionality.

summary: Fill out DocumentIterator findByteRange and filter
description:
findByteRange - Finds the key in the underlying buffer, and returns the [startIndex, endIndex) containing the corresponding element (includes from beginning of key to end of value).
filter - filter key value pairs from a document

summary: Add appname option to MongoClientOptions
description: We support this via connection string and we should add it to the client options as well.

summary: Add replicaSet option to MongoClientOptions
Fix max BSON size limit to be Int32.max rather than server's default max BSON size
description: Currently, if a user sets a value in a {{BSONDocument}} via the subscript, if that new element would cause the document to exceed the server's default max BSON size (i.e. 16MB), the driver crashes because subscripts can't throw. This size should be modified to be the max size the BSON format itself can support (since swift-bson is a standalone BSON library), which is {{Int32.max}}, since each document begins with a length stored as an Int32. This also matches behavior of the existing libbson-based BSON library.

summary: Determine which URI options are missing from MongoClientOptions
description:
This ticket entails going through the URI options spec and determining which options we only support via the connection string.
Depending on the number of options and the complexity of supporting them via options struct, the work to implement support for these may be split out into separate tickets.

summary: Add timestamp property to BSONObjectID
description: We don't expose the timestamp, but per conversation on DRIVERS-1302, we should.

summary: Add a bson corpus test with invalid type for $code when $scope is also present
description: See DRIVERS-1326 for updated details.

summary: Add heartbeatFrequencyMS option to MongoClientOptions
Add serverSelectionTimeoutMS option to MongoClientOptions
Add localThresholdMS option to MongoClientOptions
Error if minPoolSize option is provided in connection string
description:
While libmongoc does provide a minPoolSize option, it does not behave as users might expect and should not be used:
http://mongoc.org/libmongoc/current/mongoc_client_pool_min_size.html

We should error if the option has been provided by a user in a connection string. We can check this via {{mongoc_uri_has_option}}.

summary: Add compressors option to MongoClientOptions
Add zLibCompressionLevel option to MongoClientOptions
Expectations of listIndexNames.json should refer to command as listIndexes
description: See DRIVERS-748 for updated details.

summary: Add tlsInsecure option to MongoClientOptions
